title: Yuliia Karpets. The Archaeology of the Absent and the Pictorial Intertwinings of Karina Synytsia
excerpt: The artist explores the interpenetration of extinction and loss across the dimensions of the planet, art, and individual cities, as well as within intimate environments.

title: Dmytro Novak. What Lies Behind the Laws? Philosophy as the Shadow and Conscience of Law
excerpt: The philosophy of law is born precisely in this space—between what is written in the law and what a person perceives as just.

title: Mladen Dolar. The Future of Authority?
excerpt: Modernity is believed to have dealt a fatal blow to authority, or at least to those elements of it that lack proper grounds or justification.

title: Art After Catastrophe. Goya and the Language of Pain
excerpt: How painting becomes both a witness and an accuser—a conversation about images of violence, memory, and responsibility.

title: Dance of Death. Medieval Allegory in the Modern World
excerpt: From Holbein's engravings to modern media, the image of death inviting one to dance has not vanished; it has merely changed its costume.

title: The Power of the Uniform. What Does Clothing Say About Authority?
excerpt: A uniform is not just clothing. It is a language that the body speaks to other bodies: obey, fear, respect.

title: Oksana Zabuzhko. The Geography of Memory and the Topology of Trauma
excerpt: Exploring how collective trauma maps itself onto physical landscapes, altering the architecture of cities and the subconscious of generations.
imageAlt: Foggy city landscape with old architecture

title: The Architecture of Silence. Monastic Spaces and Modern Noise
excerpt: A deep dive into how intentional structural void and physical isolation serve as the ultimate resistance against the hyper-connected world.

title: Giorgio Agamben. The Sovereign and the Threshold of Exception
excerpt: When the suspension of law becomes the ultimate expression of the law itself: an analysis of the modern political state of emergency.

title: The Tyranny of the Screen. Transparency as a Form of Control
excerpt: In a world demanding absolute transparency, the right to opacity becomes the final frontier for individual freedom and genuine privacy.
imageAlt: Matrix of green digital code on a dark display

title: In the Shadow of the Guillotine. Public Execution as Theater
excerpt: An examination of how historical mechanisms of state violence were intentionally choreographed to transform terror into popular entertainment.
imageAlt: Dark dramatic stage lighting in an empty theater

title: The Anatomy of Nostalgia. Why We Long for Timelines We Never Lived
excerpt: An essay investigating the cultural phenomenon of anemoia—the acute, paradoxical yearning for a past one has never personally witnessed.
imageAlt: Stack of old vintage books and photographs
